designPrinciples.summary: Every card earns its space. Illustrations create identity. Color communicates, not decorates. Motion orients the user. Fast over slow. Content should never jump or reflow unexpectedly.
principles: Every card earns its space. If a card does not contain content the user needs on this page, remove it. Visual interest comes from the quality and variety of a few well-designed cards, not from filling every pixel.
Illustrations create identity. A user should identify which section they are looking at by the illustration alone, without reading the title.
Color communicates, not decorates. Every use of color carries meaning — status, category, or hierarchy. Color is never applied purely for visual variety.
Motion should feel natural, not performative. Animations exist to orient the user, provide feedback, and create continuity. If an animation does not serve one of those purposes, remove it.
Fast over slow. Most transitions land in the 200–400ms range. Anything over 500ms feels sluggish. Users should never wait for an animation to finish.
Content should never jump or reflow unexpectedly. Use drawers over inline panels, stacked carousels over expanding rows. The user's spatial map of the page must remain stable.
Avoid the trending default. If a pattern is in every SaaS template and dashboard kit, evaluate whether it serves our users or just follows convention.

iconCatalog.description: Master registry of all branded card illustrations. Each is permanently assigned, never swapped or reused. Full spec: specs/BRANDED-CARD-ILLUSTRATION-REGISTRY.md
iconCatalog.guidelines: Assignments are permanent. No duplicates. Pattern family enforcement by lifecycle phase. No two adjacent cards share accent color. Every illustration gets a creative codename. Dark mode required from day one. SVGs only — clean, no embedded rasters, viewBox set.

constructionReference.layer1: Tinted Container: 80–96px, border-radius 16–20px, accent color at 6–10% opacity
constructionReference.layer2: Decorative Pattern: 3–12% opacity geometric texture (circles, dots, hexagons, orbits, waves)
constructionReference.layer3: Primary Icon: Custom SVG, accent color at varied opacities, minimum one micro-detail

darkAdaptation.container: White at 5–8% opacity (instead of accent tint)

buildPriority.phase1: Dashboard Essentials: The Launchpad, The Thermometer, The Radar, The Agenda
buildPriority.phase2: Production Apps: The Compass (HomeReady), The Finish Line (HomeStretch), The Hearth (The Oven), The Press (Newsletter Studio)
buildPriority.phase3: Rello Modules: The Runway, The Blueprint, The Web, The Tracker
buildPriority.phase4: New Apps: The Welcome Mat, The Fields, The Signal, Command Center, The Observatory
buildPriority.phase5: Admin & Broker: Engine identity cards + Broker-specific cards

five-before-9.name: Five Before 9
five-before-9.iconDescription: Play/dial button with forward motion lines
five-before-9.microDetail: Small pulse ring on button corner

the-pulse.name: The Pulse (Active Deals)
the-pulse.iconDescription: EKG heartbeat line with an alert badge, suggesting activity and vitality in the pipeline
the-pulse.backgroundPattern: Wave pattern — flowing sine-wave lines behind the heartbeat

morning-brew.name: Morning Brew (Market Intel)
morning-brew.assignedCard: Morning Brew — daily market intelligence update
morning-brew.iconDescription: Coffee cup with steam wisps rising, warm and inviting. Cup has a two-tone fill (cream top, warm base) with a rounded handle
morning-brew.backgroundPattern: Dot cluster — small evenly-spaced dots in a soft grid behind the cup, with steam wisps as accent marks
morning-brew.notes: The coffee cup is the most recognizable icon in the system. Steam wisps give it personality.

the-vault.name: The Vault (Contacts)
the-vault.assignedCard: The Vault — contact database and management
the-vault.iconDescription: Target with crosshairs — concentric rings with a center dot, suggesting precision and targeting
the-vault.backgroundPattern: Concentric ring pattern — rings radiating outward from the crosshair center

todays-agenda.name: Today's Agenda
todays-agenda.assignedCard: Today's Agenda — daily schedule and tasks
todays-agenda.iconDescription: Calendar with date dots and clip bindings at top. Interior shows a grid of dots representing scheduled items, with one dot highlighted
todays-agenda.backgroundPattern: Grid pattern — evenly spaced small dots in a calendar grid formation
todays-agenda.notes: The purple accent distinguishes it from other blue-toned icons

the-scoreboard.name: The Scoreboard
the-scoreboard.assignedCard: The Scoreboard — performance metrics and GCI
the-scoreboard.iconDescription: Star with orbital sparkles — a five-pointed star outline with small orbital dots and a dashed circular orbit ring
the-scoreboard.backgroundPattern: Dashed orbit lines — circular dashed strokes creating an orbital feel around the star

compliance-shield.backgroundPattern: Hexagonal tessellation — small hexagon shapes in a honeycomb arrangement

pipeline-rocket.backgroundPattern: Dot-grid field — matrix of evenly-spaced dots creating a starfield or grid behind the rocket

pipeline-thermometer-icon.iconDescription: Thermometer with a heat gradient fill (cold blue at bottom to hot red at top) and small heat waves at the top

pipeline-thermometer-icon.accentColor: Gradient from cold (blue/gray) through warming to hot (red)

hot-leads-alert.assignedCard: Hot Leads Alert — urgent hot lead notification card
hot-leads-alert.iconDescription: Flame with a notification bell overlapping, suggesting urgency and active alerts
hot-leads-alert.backgroundPattern: Heat waves — curved lines rising upward like heat distortion

agent-partners.name: Agent Partners
agent-partners.assignedCard: Agent Partners — partner agent network and collaboration
agent-partners.iconDescription: Two person silhouettes connected by a line with a building/office icon between them
agent-partners.backgroundPattern: Network nodes — scattered dots connected by thin lines in a loose network

antiPatterns: No colored side borders on cards. Use dot indicators, tinted backgrounds, branded illustrations, or status badges instead.
No generic icon library icons as primary card visuals. Library icons are fine for navigation, buttons, and inline indicators — but card-level identity requires custom SVG illustrations.
No inline detail panels that push or compress content. Always use overlay drawers.
No uniform card sizing. Every dashboard page uses at least two different card tiers.
No emoji as icons. Emoji render differently across OS, cannot be color-controlled, and don't support decorative backgrounds.

drawer.name: Drawer (Side Panel)
drawer.description: A right-anchored overlay panel that slides in from the right edge of the viewport to show detail views. Overlays the main content without displacing it. A scrim dims the underlying content to signal focus.
drawer.behavior: Slides in from right (translateX: 100% to 0). Duration: 250–350ms. Easing: ease-in-out or cubic-bezier(0.4, 0.0, 0.2, 1). Scrim fades to 30–40% black. Closes via X button, scrim click, or Escape key. Closing reverses the animation. Main content beneath remains exactly as the user left it — no shifting, no resizing.
drawer.appearance: Width: 400–520px (or ~35–40% of viewport). Background: card-background color. Border-left with card-border color. Shadow-xl on the left edge. Z-index above all main content but below system modals. Scrim is semi-transparent black overlay covering the full viewport.
drawer.doUse: Lead detail views from any list, table, or card. File/document preview. Notification detail. Any item-level drill-down where the user clicks to see more about a specific item in a list.
drawer.dontUse: Full-page forms with significant data entry — use a full-page view or centered modal instead. Multi-step wizards or step-by-step flows — these should live in their own full-screen view, not stacked inside a drawer.

toast.name: Toast Notification
toast.description: A small, non-blocking message that appears briefly to confirm an action or surface a notification, then auto-dismisses. Does not require user interaction to close.
toast.behavior: Slides in from the top-right or bottom-right. Auto-dismisses after 4–5 seconds. Can be manually dismissed by clicking an X or swiping. Multiple toasts stack vertically with 8px gap. New toasts push older ones down. Duration: 200–300ms for enter/exit animation.
toast.appearance: Background: card-background with shadow-lg. Border-radius: radius-lg. Padding: 12px 16px. Max-width: 400px. Includes an icon (success/warning/error/info), a title, and optional description text. Status color accent on the left icon.
toast.doUse: Action confirmations (saved, deleted, sent). Background process completions. Non-critical notifications that don't need immediate attention.
toast.dontUse: Critical errors that require user action — use a dialog instead. Long messages or complex content. Anything that needs the user to make a decision.

skeleton.name: Skeleton Loader
skeleton.description: Placeholder shapes with a shimmer animation shown while real content loads. Preserves the layout structure so the page doesn't jump when content arrives.
skeleton.behavior: Renders immediately on mount, before data loads. A subtle shimmer (light gradient) sweeps left-to-right across the skeleton shapes on a loop. When data arrives, skeleton fades out and real content fades in (200ms crossfade). Skeleton shapes match the approximate size and position of the real content they replace.
skeleton.appearance: Shapes use neutral-100 as the base fill. Shimmer highlight is neutral-50 at 60% opacity, sweeping across. Border-radius matches the real content (rounded rectangles for text lines, circles for avatars). Animate shimmer with 1.5–2s duration, infinite loop.
skeleton.doUse: Initial page loads. Tab switches where new data is being fetched. Any component that has a loading state longer than 300ms. Card content, table rows, profile sections, stat values.
skeleton.dontUse: Instant state changes (optimistic updates). Content that is already cached/available. Interactive elements that should be disabled instead of replaced.

card.name: Card
card.description: The primary content container in the Rello platform. Cards come in four tiers of visual hierarchy: Hero (Tier 1), Feature (Tier 2), Stat Pill (Tier 3), and Utility (Tier 4). Every dashboard uses a mix of tiers to create a natural reading order.
card.behavior: Cards mount with staggered entrance animation (see Staggered Entrance). Hoverable cards transition shadow on hover (200ms). Clickable cards show pointer cursor and open detail drawers. Feature Cards include Branded Card Illustrations in the header area.
card.appearance: Tier 1 Hero: Full-width or two-thirds, dark gradient or bold color background, large branded illustration, primary metric, call-to-action. One per view. Tier 2 Feature: White background with subtle border, branded illustration in top-right header, section title and subtitle, primary content area. Two-column layout typical. Tier 3 Stat Pill: Compact single-metric card with small icon in 36–40px tinted container. Rows of 4–6. White background, subtle border. Tier 4 Utility: Smallest cards in 5-column grid. Large branded illustration filling top third, title and tagline below.
card.doUse: Every dashboard page should use at least two card tiers. Start with one Hero Card at top, follow with Stat Pills for context, then Feature Cards for main content, optionally Utility Cards for hub navigation.
card.dontUse: Never use a grid of identically-sized cards — this creates a flat layout with no hierarchy. Never use colored side borders on cards — use dot indicators, tinted backgrounds, or branded illustrations instead.

icon-catalog.name: Icon Catalog
icon-catalog.description: The complete catalog of branded card illustrations used across the Rello platform. Each icon is a custom-drawn SVG (72–100px) with a unique decorative background pattern, permanently assigned to a specific section. These are the visual identity of each card — a user should recognize the section by the icon alone.
icon-catalog.behavior: Icons are static — no animation on the icons themselves. The card's staggered entrance handles motion. On dark backgrounds, icons adapt to stroke-only outlines at reduced opacity. Each icon is permanently assigned and never swapped between sections.
icon-catalog.appearance: 3-layer system: Layer 1 is a tinted container (rounded rectangle, 80–96px, accent color at 6–10% opacity). Layer 2 is a decorative background pattern (concentric circles, dot grids, hexagons, orbits, or waves at 3–12% opacity). Layer 3 is the primary custom SVG icon using the section's accent color at varied opacities for depth, with at least one micro-detail (sparkle, steam wisp, pulse ring).
icon-catalog.doUse: Every Tier 2 Feature Card header. Tier 4 Utility Cards (large, filling top third). Tier 1 Hero Cards (adapted for dark backgrounds). Anywhere a section needs visual identification at a glance.
icon-catalog.dontUse: Never substitute with generic icon library icons for card-level identity. Never reuse across sections. Never swap assigned illustrations. Toolbar icons and inline UI indicators should use Iconoir, not these.

branded-card-illustration.name: Branded Card Illustration
branded-card-illustration.description: A large (72–100px) custom-drawn SVG illustration with three visual layers that serves as the primary visual identifier on a content card. Every major section of the platform is assigned a permanent illustration.
branded-card-illustration.behavior: Static on mount — no animation on the illustration itself (the card's staggered entrance handles motion). On dark card backgrounds, the illustration adapts: tinted container becomes very subtle white at 5–8% opacity, decorative pattern renders in accent color at 10–20%, primary icon renders as stroke-only outline at 40–60% opacity.
branded-card-illustration.appearance: Layer 1 — Tinted Container: Rounded rectangle (border-radius 16–20px, 80–96px size), filled with card accent color at 6–10% opacity. Positioned top-right of card header, vertically aligned with title block. Layer 2 — Decorative Background: Low-opacity (3–12%) geometric pattern inside the container — concentric circles, dot grids, hexagons, orbital rings, or dashed orbits. Reads as texture, not noise. Layer 3 — Primary Icon: Custom SVG using the card's accent color at varied opacities for depth. Includes at least one micro-detail (sparkle, wave, wisp, ring). Feels illustrative, not utilitarian.
branded-card-illustration.doUse: Every Tier 2 Feature Card must include a Branded Card Illustration in its header. Tier 4 Utility Cards use a large illustration filling the top third. Tier 1 Hero Cards include a branded illustration adapted for dark backgrounds.
branded-card-illustration.dontUse: Never substitute with generic icon library icons (Lucide, Heroicons, etc.) for card-level identity. Never reuse the same illustration across different sections. Never swap assigned illustrations between cards.

stacked-carousel.name: Stacked Carousel
stacked-carousel.description: A browsable stack of cards showing one item at a time with visible depth cues — like a stack of papers on a desk. Shadow cards peek out behind the active card to communicate that more items exist.
stacked-carousel.behavior: Active card is fully visible in foreground. Two shadow cards peek out behind, each offset and smaller. User navigates with arrow buttons (bottom-right) and dot indicators (bottom-left). Card counter shows position (e.g., '2/4') in top-right. Advancing to next card: current card animates out, next card animates in. Transition: crossfade or slide, 250–300ms, ease-in-out.
stacked-carousel.appearance: Active card: full size, border-radius 20–22px, z-index top. Shadow card 1: offset 4px inward from each edge, 2px down, 80% opacity. Shadow card 2: offset 8px inward, 4px down, 50% opacity. Shadow card colors: neutral warm tones (bone, light warm gray). Navigation arrows: 32px, rounded, bordered. Dot indicators: active dot is wider (20px) with accent color, inactive dots are default size. Card counter: muted text.
stacked-carousel.doUse: Browsing 2–6 items of the same type without leaving the current view. Active deals, lead batches, reports, pipeline sessions.
stacked-carousel.dontUse: Mixed content types — never stack cards of different types. Single items — show a regular card, not a stack. More than 6 items — use a scrollable list with detail drawer instead. The stack metaphor breaks down when users can't gauge depth.

button.name: Button
button.description: The primary interactive element for triggering actions. Comes in 7 variants (primary, secondary, accent, ghost, danger, outline, link) and 8 sizes (xs through xl, plus 3 icon sizes).
button.behavior: Hover: slight opacity reduction (0.9) on solid variants, background tint on ghost/outline. Focus: ring-2 with brand-primary color. Active: slight scale reduction. Loading state: shows spinner, disables interaction. Disabled: 50% opacity, no pointer events. Transitions: 150ms ease for all interactive states.
button.appearance: Primary: brand-primary background, white text. Accent: brand-accent background, white text. Secondary: neutral-100 background, neutral text. Ghost: transparent, text only, hover shows background. Danger: error color background, white text. Outline: white background, border, neutral text. Link: brand-primary text, underlined. All variants use rounded-md (8px) corners and font-ui family.
button.doUse: Form submissions, dialog confirmations, navigation triggers, calls-to-action. Use primary for the main action, secondary or ghost for supporting actions, danger for destructive actions.
button.dontUse: Navigation that should be a link. Toggles that should be switches. Status indicators that should be badges.

badge.name: Badge
badge.description: Small pill-shaped labels that communicate status, category, or pipeline stage. Available in 12 variants including 5 pipeline stages (hot, qualified, engaged, warming, cold) and 4 status types (success, warning, error, info).
badge.behavior: Static — no animation on mount or interaction. Optional dot indicator pulses subtly for active statuses. Pipeline badges use their assigned colors consistently across all views.
badge.appearance: Pill-shaped (rounded-full). Light tinted background with solid text in the variant color. Sizes: xs (10px text), sm (12px), md (12px, more padding), lg (14px). Optional leading dot indicator (1.5px circle in current color). Optional leading icon (12px). Font-ui family, medium weight.
badge.doUse: Pipeline stage indicators on lead cards and tables. Status labels (active, pending, overdue). Category tags. Counts or quantities. Use dot indicators for statuses that represent live/active state.
badge.dontUse: Long text content — keep to 1–2 words. Navigation or clickable elements — badges are display only. Colored side borders on cards — use badges inline instead.

input.name: Input / Form Fields
input.description: Text input fields with optional labels, hints, error messages, and icons. The foundation of all form interactions in the platform.
input.behavior: Focus: border transitions to brand-primary, ring-2 appears at 20% opacity. Error state: border and ring switch to error color. Disabled: 50% opacity, neutral-50 background, no pointer events. Labels sit above the field with 6px gap. Hint text appears below in neutral-500. Error text replaces hint text in error color.
input.appearance: Height: 36px (h-9). White background, neutral-200 border, rounded-md corners. Text: 14px in foreground color. Placeholder: neutral-400. Shadow-xs. Icons (optional): positioned absolutely at 12px from left or right edge, neutral-400 color. When icon is present, input padding adjusts to 40px on that side.
input.doUse: All single-line text entry. Email, phone, name, search, URL fields. Always include a label for accessibility unless the context is unambiguous (e.g., a lone search field with an icon).
input.dontUse: Multi-line content — use Textarea instead. Selection from a fixed set — use Select instead. Binary choices — use Checkbox or Toggle instead.

dialog.name: Dialog / Modal
dialog.description: A centered overlay that demands user attention for confirmations, short forms, or critical decisions. Blocks interaction with the underlying page until dismissed.
dialog.behavior: Opens with fade-in (opacity 0 to 1) and subtle zoom (scale 0.95 to 1), 200ms. Overlay scrim fades to 50% black. Closes via X button (top-right), clicking scrim, or Escape key. Closing reverses animations. Focus is trapped inside the dialog while open.
dialog.appearance: Max-width: 32rem (lg). Centered vertically and horizontally. White background, card-border, rounded-lg corners, shadow-lg. Padding: 24px. Header: title (18px semibold) + description (14px neutral-500). Footer: buttons right-aligned, gap-2. On mobile: max-width is viewport minus 32px.
dialog.doUse: Delete confirmations. Critical decisions that require explicit user choice. Short forms (1–3 fields) that should feel modal. Alerts that require acknowledgment.
dialog.dontUse: Detail views — use a Drawer instead. Long forms or multi-step flows — use a full page. Non-critical information — use a Toast instead. Content browsing — dialogs block the page, which frustrates exploration.

table.name: Table
table.description: A structured data display for lists of leads, contacts, deals, or any homogeneous data set. Supports sorting, row click for detail drill-down, loading skeleton, and empty states.
table.behavior: Header cells with sortable columns show a sort indicator (arrow) in brand-primary on the active sort column. Clicking a sortable header toggles asc/desc. Rows highlight on hover (neutral-50 background, 150ms transition). Clickable rows show pointer cursor and open a Drawer on click. Loading state shows skeleton rows with shimmer. Empty state shows centered message.
table.appearance: White background with rounded-xl corners and neutral-100 border. Header row: neutral-50 background, neutral-600 text, 12px uppercase tracking-wider. Body rows: 14px text in foreground color, divided by neutral-100 borders. Cell padding: 12px horizontal, 12px vertical. No outer border between header and body — just the background color difference.
table.doUse: Any list of 5+ homogeneous items. Lead lists, contact directories, deal pipelines, activity logs, transaction histories.
table.dontUse: Heterogeneous content — use cards instead. Fewer than 5 items — a simple list or card layout is better. Highly visual data — use cards with branded illustrations.

empty-state.name: Empty State
empty-state.description: A centered placeholder shown when a list, table, or content area has no data. Communicates that the empty state is intentional and provides a path forward.
empty-state.behavior: Mounts with a subtle fade-in (200ms). If the empty state includes an action button, the button uses the primary variant. The icon (if provided) sits in a large neutral-100 circle for visual weight.
empty-state.appearance: Centered vertically and horizontally within its container. Icon container: 64px circle with neutral-100 background, icon in neutral-400. Title: 18px semibold. Description: 14px neutral-500, max-width 24rem. Action button below with 16px gap. Generous vertical padding (48px top and bottom).
empty-state.doUse: Empty tables, empty search results, first-time views with no data yet, empty inboxes or notification lists.
empty-state.dontUse: Error states — use an error message or dialog instead. Loading states — use skeleton loaders. Partially loaded content — show what you have.

pagination.name: Pagination
pagination.description: Page navigation for long lists of data. Shows current position, total pages, and provides direct page access with ellipsis for large ranges.
pagination.behavior: Previous/next arrows are disabled at the boundaries (page 1 and last page). Current page button uses brand-primary background with white text. Ellipsis appears when there are gaps in the page range. Clicking a page number triggers immediate navigation. Shows 'Showing page X of Y (Z total)' text.
pagination.appearance: White background, border-top with neutral-100. Buttons: 32px min-width, 32px height, rounded-lg. Active page: brand-primary background, white text. Inactive pages: neutral-600 text, hover shows neutral-100 background. Arrows: 16px icons in neutral-600, disabled state is neutral-300. Total text: 14px neutral-500.
pagination.doUse: Tables and lists with more than 20 items. Any paginated API response. Search results.
pagination.dontUse: Short lists — show all items. Infinite scroll contexts. Carousel navigation — use carousel dots instead.

staggered-entrance.name: Staggered Entrance Animation
staggered-entrance.description: A choreographed mount animation where cards and content blocks fade in and slide upward sequentially, creating a cascading waterfall effect that gives the interface spatial awareness and rhythm.
staggered-entrance.behavior: Each card begins invisible (opacity: 0) and positioned 16–24px below its final resting place. On mount, cards simultaneously fade in and slide upward. Multiple cards stagger their start times with 50–100ms delay between each. The first card anchors attention, subsequent cards flow in naturally. Total stagger time for a group should stay under 800ms (e.g., 6 cards at 100ms = last card starts at 500ms, finishes around 800–900ms).
staggered-entrance.appearance: Starting state: opacity: 0, translateY: 16–24px. Ending state: opacity: 1, translateY: 0. Duration per card: 300–400ms. Easing: ease-out or cubic-bezier(0.0, 0.0, 0.2, 1). The animation should feel like cards are rising gently into place, not snapping or bouncing.
staggered-entrance.doUse: Page/view transitions when navigating via sidebar or primary navigation. Dashboard initial loads. Tab switches within the same view. Any time new card-level content replaces existing content.
staggered-entrance.dontUse: Inline data updates within already-visible cards (e.g., a score updating). Micro-interactions on small elements (buttons, toggles). Content that is being added incrementally to an existing list (e.g., infinite scroll new items).

page-transition.name: Page Transition
page-transition.description: The overall animation pattern when navigating between top-level views or routes. Provides continuity and spatial context as the user moves through the application.
page-transition.behavior: Outgoing view fades out (opacity 1 to 0, 150ms). Brief pause (50ms). Incoming view content uses staggered entrance animation. The transition should feel like turning a page, not teleporting. Route changes in the sidebar trigger this pattern. Back navigation reverses the direction hint if applicable.
page-transition.appearance: Outgoing: opacity fades to 0 over 150ms with ease-in. Incoming: handled by staggered entrance animation on the new view's cards. No horizontal sliding of the entire page — individual cards handle their own entrance. The sidebar and header remain static during transitions.
page-transition.doUse: All top-level route changes triggered by sidebar navigation. Switching between major sections of the app.
page-transition.dontUse: Tab switches within the same view (those use staggered entrance only, no fade-out). Drawer open/close. Dialog open/close. In-page filtering or sorting.

survey-step.name: Survey Step Card
survey-step.description: A step-by-step questionnaire card used in consumer-facing intake flows (Home Ready, Home Stretch). Each step features a large branded card illustration left-justified with the question title and helper text to the right, a progress bar, a selection grid, and a live score/feedback footer.
survey-step.behavior: App name displayed above the card in the background area for context. Each question is a full card that transitions to the next on selection. Back chevron with label appears inside the card above the progress bar (visible after step 1). Progress bar animates smoothly as user advances. Selecting an option highlights it with the step's accent color border and light background, then auto-advances to the next question after 300–400ms. Score/feedback footer updates in real-time as answers are collected. The card body transitions with a subtle fade + slide (300ms ease-out). Each step has its own accent color and decorative background pattern from the branded illustration system.
survey-step.appearance: Background: app name (18px bold, neutral-300, heading font) centered above the card. Card: white background, rounded-2xl (20px), shadow-md, centered on page with max-width ~512px. Back chevron: 16px icon + 'Back' label, neutral-400 text, top of card above progress bar. Progress bar: 8px height, rounded-full, gradient fill using the step's accent color, neutral-100 track, percentage text centered below. Illustration: 88px branded card illustration left-justified — tinted container (accent at 8% opacity), decorative SVG pattern (7% opacity), and a custom themed icon matching the question topic. Title + helper sit to the right of the illustration: heading font, 20–24px, bold, left-aligned; helper 14px, neutral-500. Option grid: 2-column layout, 10px gap. Option buttons: rounded-xl (12px), border-2, py-12px px-16px, 14px medium text. Selected state: accent color border, accent-light background, accent text. Footer: rounded-xl border, centered text in accent color, 14px medium.
survey-step.doUse: Home Ready intake questionnaire. Home Stretch qualification flow. Any consumer-facing multi-step survey or assessment. Lead scoring questionnaires. Pre-approval intake forms.
survey-step.dontUse: Back-office forms (Rello admin) — use standard form fields instead. Single-question forms — use a simple input or select. Complex forms with many fields per step — use a full-page form layout.

select.name: Select / Dropdown
select.description: A dropdown selector built on Radix UI for choosing from a list of options. Used for pipeline stage selectors, audience pickers, assignee selectors, and any fixed-option selection.
select.behavior: Click trigger to open dropdown. Content appears below with fade-in + zoom animation. Selected item shows a check indicator. Supports groups with labels and separators. Keyboard navigation (arrow keys, enter, escape). Focus ring on trigger matches brand-primary.
select.appearance: Trigger: same height and styling as Input (h-9, border, rounded-md). Down arrow icon at right edge, 50% opacity. Dropdown content: white background, shadow-md, rounded-md, neutral-200 border. Items: 14px text, py-6px px-8px, hover shows neutral-100 background. Group labels: 12px neutral-500 uppercase. Two sizes: sm (h-8, 12px text) and md (h-9, 14px text).
select.doUse: Pipeline stage filters. Agent assignment. Status selection. Any choice from 3–15 options. Anywhere a native <select> would be used.
select.dontUse: Fewer than 3 options — use radio buttons. More than 15 options — use a searchable combobox. Multi-select — use checkboxes or a tag input.

textarea.name: Textarea
textarea.description: Multi-line text input for longer content — contact notes, email bodies, property descriptions, feedback.
textarea.behavior: Same focus, error, disabled states as Input. Resizable vertically (resize-y). Min-height: 80px. Labels, hints, and error messages follow the same pattern as Input.
textarea.appearance: White background, neutral-200 border, rounded-md, shadow-xs. Padding: 8px 12px. Text: 14px body font. Placeholder: neutral-400. Focus: brand-primary border + ring. Error: error color border + ring.
textarea.doUse: Contact notes. Email/SMS message bodies. Property descriptions. Any text longer than one line. Feedback forms.
textarea.dontUse: Single-line input — use Input. Structured data — use specific form fields. Rich text — use a rich text editor component.

checkbox-radio.name: Checkbox & Radio Group
checkbox-radio.description: Checkbox for multi-select choices, Radio for single-select from a group. Built on Radix UI for accessibility. Used in settings, filters, and preference screens.
checkbox-radio.behavior: Checkbox: toggles between checked/unchecked. Checked state fills with brand-primary and shows a white check icon. Radio: selecting one deselects others in the group. Both support disabled state (50% opacity). Focus ring in brand-primary. Groups use fieldset/legend for accessibility.
checkbox-radio.appearance: Checkbox: 16px square, rounded-[4px], neutral-300 border, white background. Checked: brand-primary fill, white check icon. Radio: 16px circle, neutral-300 border, white background. Selected: brand-primary fill with white inner dot. Labels: 14px medium text, 8px gap from control. Group spacing: 12px between items.
checkbox-radio.doUse: Settings and preferences (checkbox). Notification preferences (checkbox). Multi-select filters (checkbox). Single-choice questions (radio). Pipeline stage selection (radio).
checkbox-radio.dontUse: Binary on/off — use Toggle instead. Large option lists (>8) — use Select. Single-line inline toggles — use Toggle.

toggle-switch.name: Toggle / Switch
toggle-switch.description: An on/off switch for binary settings. More visually immediate than a checkbox — the user can see the current state at a glance.
toggle-switch.behavior: Click or keyboard to toggle between on and off. Transition: thumb slides left/right over 150ms with ease. On state: track fills with brand-primary. Off state: track is neutral-200. Disabled: 50% opacity. Can include an inline label describing the setting.
toggle-switch.appearance: Track: 36px wide, 20px tall, rounded-full. Thumb: 16px circle, white, shadow-xs. On: brand-primary track, thumb right. Off: neutral-200 track, thumb left. Label: 14px text to the right, 8px gap.
toggle-switch.doUse: Feature enable/disable. Notification preferences. Visibility toggles. Settings that take effect immediately. Dark mode toggle.
toggle-switch.dontUse: Multi-option choices — use checkbox or radio. Form fields that require a submit — use checkbox. Anything that isn't a clear binary.

date-picker.name: Date Picker
date-picker.description: Calendar-based date selection critical for real estate — closing dates, follow-up scheduling, showing times, listing expirations. Can be single date, date range, or date+time.
date-picker.behavior: Click input to open calendar popover. Calendar shows month grid with today highlighted. Navigate months with arrows. Click a date to select and close. Date range mode: click start, then end. Selected dates show brand-primary background. Today shows a dot indicator. Keyboard navigable.
date-picker.appearance: Input trigger: same styling as Select trigger (h-9, border, calendar icon right). Calendar popover: white background, shadow-lg, rounded-lg. Month header: heading font, 14px, with left/right arrows. Day cells: 32px squares, 14px text. Selected: brand-primary background, white text. Today: small dot below date. Range: brand-primary-light fill between start and end.
date-picker.doUse: Closing dates. Follow-up scheduling. Showing date/time selection. Listing expiration dates. Contract deadlines. Any date input in the platform.
date-picker.dontUse: Dates that are far in the past (birth dates) — use a text input with format hint. Relative dates ('in 3 days') — use a select with preset options.

file-upload.name: File Upload / Drag-and-Drop
file-upload.description: Upload area for documents, images, and listing photos. Supports drag-and-drop and click-to-browse. Shows upload progress and file previews.
file-upload.behavior: Drag files over the zone to see a highlighted drop state. Click to open file browser. Files show upload progress (progress bar). Completed files show a thumbnail preview (images) or file icon (documents). Multiple files supported. Remove individual files with an X button. Validates file type and size.
file-upload.appearance: Drop zone: dashed border (neutral-300), rounded-lg, 120px min-height. Center: upload icon (neutral-400) + 'Drag files here or click to browse' text. Active drag: brand-primary dashed border, brand-primary-light background. File list: below drop zone, each file shows icon/thumbnail + name + size + progress bar + remove button.
file-upload.doUse: Listing photo uploads. Document uploads (pre-approval letters, contracts). Profile photo. Logo upload for white-label branding.
file-upload.dontUse: Single small files where a simple file input suffices. Avatar upload — use a dedicated avatar upload component. URLs — use a text input.

search-input.name: Search Input
search-input.description: Specialized text input with search icon, instant filtering behavior, and optional autocomplete dropdown. Used across the platform for contacts, properties, and content search.
search-input.behavior: Typing filters results immediately (debounced 200ms). Shows a loading spinner while searching. Clear button (X) appears when text is present. Escape clears input. Optional autocomplete dropdown shows matching results below. Enter or clicking a result navigates.
search-input.appearance: Same base styling as Input with left search icon (neutral-400). Optional autocomplete dropdown: white background, shadow-md, rounded-md below the input. Result items: 14px text, py-8px px-12px, hover neutral-100 background. Highlighted match text in brand-primary.
search-input.doUse: Contact search. Property search. Content library search. Any list filtering. Command palette / quick-find.
search-input.dontUse: Exact value entry (like entering an address) — use Input. Selection from a known fixed list — use Select.

color-picker.name: Color Picker
color-picker.description: Hex color selector for white-label branding settings. Uses react-colorful for the picker widget with a hex input for precise values.
color-picker.behavior: Click the color swatch to open the picker popover. Drag the picker to select hue and saturation. Type a hex value directly in the input. Clicking outside closes the picker. Changes apply in real-time to the preview. Supports copy/paste of hex values.
color-picker.appearance: Swatch trigger: 32px square, rounded-md, bordered, filled with current color. Popover: white background, shadow-lg, rounded-lg, 12px padding. Picker: react-colorful default (200px wide). Hex input below picker: monospace font, centered, 12px text. Label to the right of swatch: 12px text + hex value.
color-picker.doUse: White-label brand color settings. Theme customization. Newsletter template color overrides. Any place users choose a color.
color-picker.dontUse: Preset color selection (pipeline stages, status) — use Select with color swatches instead.

stat-card.name: Stat Card / KPI Card
stat-card.description: Compact metric display card (Tier 3 Stat Pill) showing a single KPI with icon, value, label, and optional trend indicator. Used on every dashboard for at-a-glance metrics — Total Contacts, Active Flows, GCI, Avg Open Rate.
stat-card.behavior: Static display — no interaction. Optional trend indicator (up/down arrow with percentage) animates in with the staggered entrance. Value can use the stat font (Mina) for emphasis. Hoverable variant shows shadow transition.
stat-card.appearance: Compact card: white background, neutral-100 border, rounded corners (radius-lg). Icon: 36–40px tinted container (accent color at 8% opacity) with small icon inside. Value: stat font (Mina), 20–24px bold, accent color. Label: 11–12px, neutral-500. Trend: 11px, success (up) or error (down) colored, with arrow. Arranged in rows of 4–6 across.
stat-card.doUse: Dashboard KPI rows. Pipeline summary stats. Campaign performance metrics. Agent scoreboard numbers. Any single-number metric that needs quick visibility.
stat-card.dontUse: Complex data — use a Feature Card or Table. Actionable content — use a regular Card with buttons. Long text — stat cards are numbers only.

tag-chip.name: Tag / Chip
tag-chip.description: Small removable labels used for contact tags, journey tags, filter selections, and any multi-value display. Distinct from Badge — tags can be added and removed by the user.
tag-chip.behavior: Click the X button to remove a tag. Adding a tag animates it in (scale from 0.8 to 1, 150ms). Removing fades out (150ms). Tags can be clickable (navigates to filtered view) or static (display only). Optional: tag input field for adding new tags with autocomplete.
tag-chip.appearance: Pill-shaped (rounded-full). Smaller than Badge — height 24px, 11–12px text, px-8px. Neutral variant: neutral-100 background, neutral-600 text. Colored variant: light tinted background matching tag category. Remove button: X icon at right edge, 50% opacity, hover full opacity. Tag group: flex-wrap with 4px gap.
tag-chip.doUse: Contact tags (buyer, seller, VIP, referral). Journey/flow tags. Active filter indicators. Selected items in a multi-select. Email recipient chips.
tag-chip.dontUse: Status indicators — use Badge. Single-select options — use radio or select. Navigation — use tabs or links.

avatar-user.name: Avatar / User Initials
avatar-user.description: Circular user representation showing a profile photo or initials fallback. Used in agent profiles, contact lists, team views, chat messages, and activity feeds.
avatar-user.behavior: Displays image if available, otherwise renders initials in a colored circle. Image loads with a brief fade-in. Fallback shows two-letter initials centered. Avatar groups overlap with -space-x-2 and ring-2 ring-white borders.
avatar-user.appearance: Circular (rounded-full). 5 sizes: xs (24px), sm (32px), md (40px), lg (48px), xl (64px). Fallback: neutral-100 background, neutral-600 text, 14px font-medium. Group: stacked with 2px white ring, overflow shows +N counter. Image: aspect-square, object-cover.
avatar-user.doUse: Contact list rows. Chat message bubbles. Team member displays. Activity feed entries. Card headers showing assignee. Anywhere a person needs visual representation.
avatar-user.dontUse: Non-person entities (companies, properties) — use an icon or thumbnail. Very large displays — use a full profile photo component.

progress-bar.name: Progress Bar
progress-bar.description: Horizontal bar indicating completion, score, or progress. Supports multiple color variants for different contexts — pipeline stages, success/warning/error states.
progress-bar.behavior: Value animates from 0 to target on mount (300ms ease-in-out). Updates transition smoothly when value changes. No interaction — display only.
progress-bar.appearance: Track: neutral-100, rounded-full. Fill: variant color, rounded-full. 3 sizes: sm (6px), md (8px), lg (12px). 6 variants: default (brand-primary), success, warning, error, hot, cold. Value can optionally be shown as text above or beside the bar.
progress-bar.doUse: Lead score visualization. Upload progress. Journey completion percentage. Campaign performance. Pipeline thermometer segments.
progress-bar.dontUse: Loading states — use Spinner or Skeleton. Binary states — use a toggle or badge. Indeterminate progress — use Spinner.

timeline.name: Timeline / Activity Feed
timeline.description: Chronological list of events showing contact activity, lead journey history, and interaction logs. Each entry has a timestamp, icon, description, and optional detail.
timeline.behavior: Entries mount with staggered entrance (subtle, 60ms stagger per item, fade + slide-left 300ms ease-out). Most recent at top. Optionally loadable (show more button or infinite scroll). Clicking an entry can open a detail drawer. Timestamps use relative time ('2h ago') with full datetime on hover.
timeline.appearance: Vertical line (neutral-100, 2px) runs down the left side. Each entry: icon circle (32px) sitting on the line, content card to the right. Icon circle: stacked background (tinted color over solid white) so the line is fully hidden behind the circle, with a 4px solid white ring (box-shadow). Event types: success, info, warning, default (neutral). Title: 14px medium text. Description: 13px neutral-500. Timestamp: 10px neutral-400. Entries separated by 16px gap. Content cards have hover shadow transition.
timeline.doUse: Contact activity history. Lead journey visualization. Deal progress log. Email/call log. Notification history.
timeline.dontUse: Structured data — use Table. Real-time feeds — use a live-updating list. Non-chronological content — use Cards.

list-item.name: List Item / Row
list-item.description: Standardized clickable list row used for contacts, properties, tasks, and any item that opens a detail drawer on click. The building block of all list views.
list-item.behavior: Hover: neutral-50 background, 150ms transition. Click: opens detail drawer. Shows pointer cursor. Optional: swipe actions on mobile. Each row can include avatar, title, subtitle, badges, timestamp, and action buttons.
list-item.appearance: Full-width, white background, divided by neutral-100 borders. Padding: 12px 16px. Title: 14px medium foreground. Subtitle: 13px neutral-500. Right side: badges, timestamps, or action icons. Avatar (if present): 36px on the left. Action buttons: ghost variant, appear on hover.
list-item.doUse: Contact lists. Property listings. Task lists. Notification items. Search results. Any homogeneous list of clickable items.
list-item.dontUse: Complex multi-column data — use Table. Visual/card-heavy displays — use Card grid. Fewer than 3 items — use Cards.

tab-navigation.name: Tab Navigation
tab-navigation.description: Horizontal tab bar for switching between sections within the same view. Used everywhere — settings pages, detail views, dashboard sub-sections.
tab-navigation.behavior: Click a tab to switch content. Active tab shows brand-primary underline or background treatment. Content transitions with staggered entrance (no page fade-out since we're staying in the same view). Keyboard navigable with arrow keys. Badge count can appear on tabs to show pending items.
tab-navigation.appearance: Tab bar: bottom border neutral-200. Tabs: 14px medium text, px-16px py-8px. Active tab: brand-primary text, 2px brand-primary bottom border. Inactive: neutral-500 text, hover neutral-700. Optional badge: small rounded count in brand-primary or error color. Tab bar sticks below the page header.
tab-navigation.doUse: Settings sub-sections. Detail view sections (overview, activity, files). Dashboard category switching. Any view with 2–7 parallel sections.
tab-navigation.dontUse: More than 7 tabs — use a sidebar or dropdown. Page-level navigation — use sidebar. Single-section views. Deep navigation — use breadcrumbs.

breadcrumbs.name: Breadcrumbs
breadcrumbs.description: Horizontal trail showing the user's current position in a deep navigation hierarchy. Helps users understand where they are and navigate back to parent views.
breadcrumbs.behavior: Each segment is a clickable link except the last (current page). Separator between segments (/ or >). Truncates long paths with ellipsis. Clicking a breadcrumb navigates to that level.
breadcrumbs.appearance: 12–13px text. Links: neutral-500, hover brand-primary. Current: neutral-700, not clickable. Separator: neutral-400, 4px horizontal gap. Positioned below the page header, above the page title.
breadcrumbs.doUse: Settings pages (Settings > Branding > Colors). Nested detail views. Multi-level navigation paths. Any page more than 2 levels deep.
breadcrumbs.dontUse: Top-level pages (Dashboard, Pipeline). Single-level views. Mobile views — use a back button instead.

dropdown-menu.name: Dropdown Menu / Context Menu
dropdown-menu.description: The 'three dots' or 'more options' menu that appears on cards, table rows, and anywhere space-constrained actions are needed.
dropdown-menu.behavior: Click trigger (three-dot icon or button) to open menu. Menu appears below/beside trigger with fade-in + slight slide. Click an item to execute action and close. Click outside or Escape to dismiss. Supports icons, separators, and destructive items (red text). Keyboard navigable.
dropdown-menu.appearance: Trigger: ghost button with three-dot icon (neutral-500). Menu: white background, shadow-lg, rounded-md, neutral-200 border, min-width 160px. Items: 14px text, py-6px px-12px. Hover: neutral-100 background. Destructive items: error color text. Separators: neutral-100 horizontal line. Icons: 16px, neutral-500, left of label.
dropdown-menu.doUse: Card overflow actions (edit, archive, delete). Table row actions. Anywhere you have 3+ actions that don't fit as visible buttons. Right-click context menus.
dropdown-menu.dontUse: Primary actions — use visible buttons. Navigation — use links or tabs. Forms — use select or radio.

stepper-wizard.name: Stepper / Wizard
stepper-wizard.description: Multi-step flow indicator showing numbered or named steps, current position, and completion state. Used for onboarding, listing launch, survey builders, and any multi-page process.
stepper-wizard.behavior: Steps show numbered circles connected by lines. Completed steps show a check icon and filled circle. Current step is highlighted. Future steps are dimmed. Clicking a completed step navigates back to it. Step content transitions with fade + slide.
stepper-wizard.appearance: Horizontal layout: circles (32px) connected by lines (neutral-200). Completed circle: brand-primary fill, white check. Current circle: brand-primary border, brand-primary text. Future circle: neutral-200 border, neutral-400 text. Connecting line: brand-primary for completed segments, neutral-200 for future. Step labels: 12px text below circles.
stepper-wizard.doUse: Agent onboarding. Listing launch wizard. Newsletter builder steps. Any process with 3–7 distinct stages that must be completed in order.
stepper-wizard.dontUse: Non-linear processes. Fewer than 3 steps — just use sequential views. More than 7 steps — break into grouped sections.

spinner.name: Spinner
spinner.description: Inline loading indicator for small async operations — button loading states, saving indicators, and brief wait moments. Different use case from Skeleton (which preserves layout).
spinner.behavior: Continuous rotation animation (CSS animate-spin). Appears instantly when loading begins, disappears when complete. Can be used inside buttons (ButtonSpinner variant) or standalone. Multiple sizes for different contexts.
spinner.appearance: Circular border with one segment in brand-primary (the rest neutral-300). Sizes: xs (12px), sm (16px), md (24px), lg (32px), xl (48px). Border width scales with size. ButtonSpinner variant: sm size with white border (for use inside colored buttons). PageLoader: centered lg spinner with message text below.
spinner.doUse: Button loading states. Small inline async operations. Brief waits (< 2 seconds). API call indicators.
spinner.dontUse: Long loading times — use Skeleton instead. Page-level loading — use PageLoader or Skeleton. Content that has a known layout — use Skeleton.

alert-banner.name: Alert / Banner
alert-banner.description: Persistent info, warning, or error message displayed at the top of a page or section. Unlike Toast (which auto-dismisses), alerts stay visible until dismissed or the condition is resolved.
alert-banner.behavior: Mounts with fade-in (200ms). Stays visible until user dismisses (X button) or the condition resolves. Dismissing animates out with fade + collapse (200ms ease-out) so remaining alerts slide up smoothly. Can include an action button (e.g., 'Retry', 'Update'). Does not auto-dismiss. Multiple alerts stack vertically.
alert-banner.appearance: Full-width bar, 12px vertical padding, 16px horizontal. 4 variants: info (info-light background, info border), success (success-light, success border), warning (warning-light, warning border), error (error-light, error border). Left icon matching variant color. Text: 14px, variant color. Dismiss X button at right edge. Optional action button.
alert-banner.doUse: Sync errors that persist. Subscription expiration warnings. System maintenance notices. Required action alerts. Feature announcements.
alert-banner.dontUse: Transient confirmations — use Toast. Inline field errors — use Input error state. Critical blocking issues — use Dialog.

confirmation-dialog.name: Confirmation Dialog
confirmation-dialog.description: Specialized modal for destructive or irreversible actions — delete, archive, cancel subscription. A focused version of Dialog with specific UX patterns for confirmation flows.
confirmation-dialog.behavior: Same animation as Dialog (fade + zoom, 200ms). Title clearly states the action. Description explains consequences. Two buttons: cancel (ghost) and confirm (danger or primary). Confirm button text describes the action ('Delete Lead', not 'OK'). Focus lands on the cancel button by default (safe default).
confirmation-dialog.appearance: Same as Dialog but narrower (max-width 28rem). Icon at top: large warning or error icon in tinted circle. Title: 18px semibold. Description: 14px neutral-600. Button row: cancel on left, destructive action on right in error color.
confirmation-dialog.doUse: Delete confirmations. Archive/remove actions. Subscription cancellations. Any action that cannot be undone. Bulk operations affecting multiple items.
confirmation-dialog.dontUse: Informational messages — use Alert or Toast. Routine saves — just save. Reversible actions — skip the confirmation.

tooltip.name: Tooltip
tooltip.description: Small hover hint that appears near an element to explain its purpose. Used for icon-only buttons, truncated text, and feature explanations.
tooltip.behavior: Appears on hover after a 300ms delay (prevents flickering). Disappears when mouse leaves. Positioned above the element by default, flips if near viewport edge. No interaction — purely informational. Touch devices: appears on long-press.
tooltip.appearance: Dark background (neutral-800), white text, 12px. Padding: 4px 8px. Rounded-md. Small arrow/caret pointing toward the trigger element. Shadow-sm. Max-width: 200px for longer tooltips. Z-index above most content (70).
tooltip.doUse: Icon-only buttons that need labels. Truncated text that has a full version. Feature explanations for new users. Data points that need additional context.
tooltip.dontUse: Rich content (images, buttons) — use a Popover. Required information — tooltips are easily missed. Mobile-primary interfaces — tooltips require hover.

divider.name: Divider / Separator
divider.description: Simple horizontal rule that separates content sections. Used within cards, between form groups, and in sidebars.
divider.appearance: 1px height, neutral-100 color, full width. Can have vertical margin (8–16px). Optional label variant: centered text ('or', 'Section Name') with lines on either side, 12px neutral-400 text.
divider.doUse: Between form field groups. Between card content sections. In dropdown menus between action groups. In sidebars between navigation groups.
divider.dontUse: Between every item in a list — use subtle border-bottom on rows instead. As a design element — dividers should be nearly invisible.

accordion.name: Accordion / Collapsible
accordion.description: Expandable content sections that show/hide detail on click. Used for FAQ pages, settings groups, and any content that benefits from progressive disclosure.
accordion.behavior: Click header to expand/collapse. Content animates using CSS grid transition (grid-template-rows 0fr → 1fr, 300ms ease-in-out) for smooth expand/collapse regardless of content height. Chevron icon rotates to indicate state. Only one section open at a time (accordion mode) or multiple can be open (collapsible mode). Keyboard accessible.
accordion.appearance: Header: full-width, py-12px px-16px. Title: 14px semibold text. Chevron: right-aligned, neutral-400, rotates 180° when open. Content area: px-16px pb-16px. Borders: neutral-100 between items. Active header: slightly darker background or brand-primary text.
accordion.doUse: FAQ sections. Settings groups. Long forms broken into sections. Feature explanations. Any content where showing everything at once is overwhelming.
accordion.dontUse: Navigation — use tabs or sidebar. Two or fewer sections — just show all content. Critical information that users must see — don't hide it.

pipeline-thermometer.name: Pipeline Thermometer
pipeline-thermometer.description: THE core visual of the Rello platform. A horizontal gradient bar showing lead temperature from Cold → Warming → Engaged → Qualified → Hot. Uses the Home Ready gradient color scheme (blue → cyan → yellow → orange → red) filling progressively based on lead stage. Stage labels with counts sit below the bar. This is the single most important visualization in the system.
pipeline-thermometer.behavior: Gradient fills progressively from left to right based on lead stage. The gradient stretches proportionally so the visible portion always starts at blue (cold) and ends at the color corresponding to the current stage. Transition: fill width animates with ease-in-out over 500ms when stage changes. A small white dot marks the current position. Stage labels below are clickable and show counts. Can be used at multiple sizes.
pipeline-thermometer.appearance: Horizontal gradient bar, rounded-full. Gradient: #93c5fd → #06b6d4 → #22d3ee → #facc15 → #fb923c → #ef4444. Fill stretches proportionally to active stage. Unfilled area: neutral-100 track. White dot (2.5px) marks current position. Stage labels below: Cold (xx), Warming (xx), Engaged (xx), Qualified (xx), Hot (xx). Size variants: compact (8px height for list rows), standard (12px for cards), large (20px for detail views). Distribution variant shows full gradient with counts below for dashboard overviews.
pipeline-thermometer.doUse: Lead detail views. Pipeline overview cards. Lead list rows. Contact cards. Any view where lead temperature is relevant. Dashboard summary stats.
pipeline-thermometer.dontUse: Non-lead data. Binary states — use a toggle or badge. Arbitrary percentages — use Progress Bar.

app-card.name: App Card
app-card.description: The specific card variant used on MLO and Broker dashboards showing app stats, status badges, descriptions, and quick actions. A specialized Tier 2/3 hybrid used for showing the status of platform applications.
app-card.behavior: Clickable — navigates to the app's full dashboard. Hover: shadow-md transition. Shows branded illustration header, app name, subtitle, status badge (Active/Pending/Inactive), and 2–3 key metrics unique to each app. Metrics use varied visual treatments per card (stat pills, progress rings, mini bars, trend arrows, tinted stat boxes) to keep the dashboard visually engaging.
app-card.appearance: White background, neutral-100 border, rounded-xl, overflow-hidden. Branded illustration header: 72px tall, accent color at 4% opacity background, decorative SVG pattern at 5% opacity, themed icon centered. Body: px-16px pb-16px pt-12px. App name: heading font, 14px semibold. Subtitle: 10px neutral-400. Status badge: rounded-full, 9px font, colored background (success 15% for Active, warning 15% for Pending, neutral for Inactive). Metrics area: varied per app — stat pills (stat font, 18px bold in accent color + 8px label), progress rings (32px SVG circle), mini bars (6px rounded-full), tinted stat boxes (accent at 7% background, rounded-lg). 3-column grid layout on dashboard.
app-card.doUse: MLO dashboard showing their apps (Home Ready, Home Stretch, Newsletter Studio). Broker dashboard showing team app status. Admin views listing platform applications.
app-card.dontUse: Generic content cards — use Card. Non-app entities. Lists with more than 8 items — use a table.

white-label-preview.name: White-Label Preview
white-label-preview.description: A live preview component that shows how the current white-label branding (primary color, accent color) looks applied to a miniature app mockup. Used in the branding settings screen so agents can see changes in real-time before saving.
white-label-preview.behavior: Updates in real-time as the user changes brand colors via the Color Picker. Shows a miniature mockup of a consumer-facing page (e.g., Home Ready landing) with the selected colors applied to buttons, links, headers, and accents. Can toggle between light and dark mockup views.
white-label-preview.appearance: Card container with a scaled-down app mockup inside (60–70% scale). Shows: a header with the primary color, a button in primary, a link in primary, an accent-colored section, and text in foreground. Border: neutral-200 dashed to indicate it's a preview. Label: 'Preview' in neutral-400 above the mockup.
white-label-preview.doUse: White-label branding settings page. Color picker confirmation. Onboarding brand setup. Any place where brand colors are being configured.
white-label-preview.dontUse: Actual app rendering — this is a mockup only. Non-branding settings.

milo-chat.name: Milo Smart Assistant Chat
milo-chat.description: Milo is Rello's Smart Assistant — a conversational interface that helps agents with market data, lead insights, daily planning, and property information. Milo is always referred to as a 'Smart Assistant', never as 'AI'.
milo-chat.behavior: Milo chat opens as a right-side panel (similar to drawer behavior) or as an expandable FAB (floating action button) in the bottom-right. Messages stream in with a typing indicator. Milo's responses use a subtle staggered entrance for multi-part answers. The chat maintains conversation context within the session. Quick-action chips appear below Milo's responses for common follow-ups.
milo-chat.appearance: Milo's avatar: the milo-circle.svg or milo-head.svg image. Message bubbles: Milo's messages use a light brand-primary-light background, user messages use neutral-100. Font: body font (Open Sans) at 14px. Chat panel width matches drawer width (400–520px). Input area at bottom with send button in brand-primary.
milo-chat.doUse: Contextual help and data retrieval. Market data queries. Lead scoring explanations. Daily planning assistance. Property comparisons and insights.
milo-chat.dontUse: Critical actions that require explicit confirmation — use a dialog. Bulk operations. Anything that modifies data without the user's explicit instruction.
